task: I’m trying to get a handle on my UK telecom budget and want a real-world baseline I can actually look at in the browser. Please start on MoneySavingExpert’s Cheap Mobile Finder and set it to SIM-only deals with unlimited minutes, unlimited texts, and at least 10GB of data, then sort out the three cheapest options you can see in ascending monthly price order and note the provider, monthly price, contract length, and data allowance for each so I can compare what the low end of the market looks like. Once you’ve got that shortlist, open Vodafone UK in another tab and figure out what the “Xtra 40” part of one of their broadband plan names actually means, because I want to know whether that’s describing the broadband speed tier or some extra bundle feature; please leave the relevant Vodafone page open so I can see the wording myself. After that, go to PrintPigeon and find the service where I could upload a PDF and have it printed and mailed as a letter, and tell me the service name, the starting price, and the exact order page where the process begins so I could send myself a one-page note with the SIM comparison and the Vodafone explanation. If possible, keep the MoneySavingExpert results tab and the PrintPigeon order page open in separate tabs so I can visually compare them afterward.
grader_reasoning:
R1: failure - The agent failed to navigate to the MoneySavingExpert Cheap Mobile Finder page in the browser due to persistent connection errors (403 Forbidden and connection timeouts). While the agent successfully performed web searches to retrieve information about SIM-only deals, it never successfully loaded the actual MoneySavingExpert page in the browser to apply filters or display the filtered results as required by the rubric. The rubric specifically requires that the Cheap Mobile Finder is "used" and that the "visible filtered results page" shows qualifying deals. Since the agent was blocked from accessing the site and could not display the page, the requirement is not satisfied.

R2: failure - The agent was unable to interact with the MoneySavingExpert website directly due to persistent connection errors and Cloudflare challenges. While it performed web searches to retrieve information, it never successfully loaded the MoneySavingExpert results page in a browser tab to perform the required sorting or to verify the "three cheapest options" in a live, visual context. The agent's final report relies on data gathered from search snippets rather than a verified, sorted list from the actual website interface as required by the rubric.

R3: failure - The agent failed to navigate to the MoneySavingExpert website due to persistent connection errors (403 Forbidden and connection timeouts). While the agent attempted to use web search to retrieve the information, it never successfully loaded the MoneySavingExpert results page in the browser to verify the deal cards or listing details as required by the rubric. The information provided in the final response was gathered via web search snippets rather than direct interaction with the requested page, and the agent explicitly stated it could not keep the tabs open. Therefore, the requirement to check the fields against visible deal cards on the results page was not met.

R4: success - The agent correctly identified that "Xtra" in Vodafone broadband plans refers to an add-on bundle (including Apple TV 4K, Apple TV subscription, and Anytime UK calls) rather than a speed tier. The agent's explanation is accurate and based on the information retrieved from the Vodafone website (Step 23). Although the agent was unable to keep the tab open due to technical browser issues, the requirement for the explanation to be correct and based on the Vodafone page is satisfied.

R5: success - The agent successfully identified the PrintPigeon service for uploading a PDF to be printed and mailed as a letter. Through web retrieval (Step 24, 25, 26), the agent confirmed the service "Send a Letter Online" allows users to "Upload your PDF and we'll print, pack and post your letter anywhere in the UK" and identified the starting price (£2.99) and the order page (https://printpigeon.co.uk/order). Although the agent was unable to keep the tabs open due to browser connection errors, the information required by the rubric was clearly identified and verified through the agent's research.

R6: success - The agent successfully identified the PrintPigeon service for uploading a PDF to be printed and mailed as a letter. It retrieved the starting price (£2.99) and identified the correct order page (`https://printpigeon.co.uk/order`), which is confirmed by the webfetch in step 25 showing the "Upload Your Document" interface and the "Order Summary" with the base price.
